reasoning: Despite recent critical media profiles and calls from progressive groups for him to resign due to behavioral and health concerns, Senator John Fetterman has adamantly stated that he will not resign. Furthermore, he has repeatedly quashed rumors of leaving the Democratic Party. The resolution date of July 1, 2025, provides a very short window (roughly one month from the May 29, 2025 knowledge cutoff) for such a drastic event to occur. Even if Fetterman were to drop his Democratic registration to become an Independent in the near future, precedent (e.g., Sinema, Manchin) suggests he would likely continue caucusing with the Democrats to maintain committee assignments. Therefore, the chance of him resigning, switching to the Republican caucus, being expelled, or passing away in the next 33 days is extremely low.
